---
title: "Benchmarks"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Benchmarks}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 5
)

library(basetable)
library(bench)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

The benchmarks below compare `basetable` with base R and `dplyr` on three
representative table operations:

- row subsetting and column selection
- keyed joins
- grouped aggregation

The goal is not to prove a universal winner. It is to show how the package
behaves on the kinds of operations it is designed for.

```{r data}
set.seed(1)

subset_n <- 1e5
merge_n <- 5e4

subset_df <- data.frame(
  id = seq_len(subset_n),
  grp = sample(letters[1:5], subset_n, replace = TRUE),
  value = rnorm(subset_n)
)

subset_tbl <- tibble::as_tibble(subset_df)

merge_x <- data.frame(
  id = seq_len(merge_n),
  value_x = rnorm(merge_n)
)

merge_y <- data.frame(
  id = sample(seq_len(merge_n), merge_n),
  value_y = rnorm(merge_n)
)

merge_x_tbl <- tibble::as_tibble(merge_x)
merge_y_tbl <- tibble::as_tibble(merge_y)
```

```{r benchmark}
run_subset_benchmark <- function() {
  bench::mark(
    basetable = basetable::subset(
      subset_df,
      value > 0,
      select = c("id", "grp", "value")
    ),
    base_r = base::subset(
      subset_df,
      value > 0,
      select = c("id", "grp", "value")
    ),
    dplyr = subset_tbl |>
      dplyr::filter(value > 0) |>
      dplyr::select(id, grp, value),
    iterations = 15,
    check = FALSE
  )
}

run_merge_benchmark <- function() {
  bench::mark(
    basetable = basetable::merge(merge_x, merge_y, by = "id"),
    base_r = base::merge(merge_x, merge_y, by = "id"),
    dplyr = dplyr::inner_join(merge_x_tbl, merge_y_tbl, by = "id"),
    iterations = 15,
    check = FALSE
  )
}

run_aggregate_benchmark <- function() {
  bench::mark(
    basetable = basetable::aggregate(
      subset_df,
      by = "grp",
      value = "value",
      fun = mean,
      sort = FALSE
    ),
    base_r = stats::aggregate(
      value ~ grp,
      data = subset_df,
      FUN = mean
    ),
    dplyr = subset_tbl |>
      dplyr::group_by(grp) |>
      dplyr::summarise(value = mean(value), .groups = "drop"),
    iterations = 15,
    check = FALSE
  )
}

summarise_benchmark <- function(x, operation) {
  out <- tibble::as_tibble(x) |>
    dplyr::transmute(
      operation = operation,
      implementation = as.character(expression),
      median_ms = as.numeric(median) * 1000,
      itr_per_sec = as.numeric(`itr/sec`),
      mem_alloc_mb = as.numeric(mem_alloc) / 1024^2
    )

  out |>
    dplyr::group_by(operation) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      relative_time = median_ms / median_ms[implementation == "basetable"],
      implementation = factor(
        implementation,
        levels = c("basetable", "base_r", "dplyr"),
        labels = c("basetable", "base R", "dplyr")
      )
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()
}

benchmark_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  summarise_benchmark(run_subset_benchmark(), "Subset and select"),
  summarise_benchmark(run_merge_benchmark(), "Merge"),
  summarise_benchmark(run_aggregate_benchmark(), "Aggregate")
) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    operation = factor(
      operation,
      levels = c("Subset and select", "Merge", "Aggregate")
    )
  )
```

## Results Table

```{r table, results='asis'}
table_results <- benchmark_results |>
  dplyr::arrange(operation, implementation) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    median_ms = format(round(median_ms, 2), nsmall = 2),
    itr_per_sec = format(round(itr_per_sec, 1), nsmall = 1),
    mem_alloc_mb = format(round(mem_alloc_mb, 2), nsmall = 2),
    relative_time = format(round(relative_time, 2), nsmall = 2)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(
    operation,
    implementation,
    median_ms,
    itr_per_sec,
    mem_alloc_mb,
    relative_time
  )

knitr::kable(
  table_results,
  col.names = c(
    "Operation",
    "Implementation",
    "Median (ms)",
    "Iterations / sec",
    "Memory (MB)",
    "Relative time vs basetable"
  ),
  align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r")
)
```

## Absolute Time

```{r absolute-plot, fig.cap="Median runtime by implementation and operation."}
ggplot(
  benchmark_results,
  aes(x = implementation, y = median_ms, fill = implementation)
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~operation, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Median time (ms, log scale)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

## Relative Time

```{r relative-plot, fig.cap="Relative runtime compared with basetable for each workload. Values above 1 mean slower than basetable."}
ggplot(
  benchmark_results,
  aes(x = implementation, y = relative_time, fill = implementation)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 2, color = "grey50") +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~operation, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.01)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Relative time vs basetable",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```
